Morgan Stanley's Zentner Urges Stock Picking, Warns on US Recession Risks
data.description:
Ellen Zentner, chief economic strategist at Morgan Stanley Wealth Management, says her firm is not "broad sellers of US equities here," with "pockets where earnings can get better, not worse." Zentner also sees an "uncomfortable" 40% chance of a US recession over the next 12 months.

Guinness Maker Diageo Names New North America Chief
data.description:
By Joshua Kirby


Diageo has appointed John O'Keeffe as new leader for its key North America business after the previous regional chief executive officer stepped down.

O'Keeffe will take over as CEO and president of Diageo North America, moving from a previous role leading the drinks giant's Asia-Pacific, India and travel businesses.

"[O'Keeffe's] blend of consumer, brand-building and category capabilities uniquely positions him to lead North America over the next few years," a spokesperson for Diageo said Wednesday.

O'Keeffe replaces former CEO Sally Grimes, who has stepped down. In a post on LinkedIn, Grimes said she would be dedicating her time to a foundation dedicated to her late son.

Grimes joined Diageo in 2023 from California snack-bar maker Clif Bar, where she was CEO.

O'Keeffe constitutes a "strong and reassuring successor" to Grimes, analysts at Barclays wrote in a note following the news.

"Given his long tenure and breadth of experience, we believe O'Keeffe is well-regarded by the investor community and is seen as a steady and credible operator," Barclays said.

The change in leadership comes as Diageo, maker of Guinness stout, Johnnie Walker whisky and Smirnoff vodka, struggles to reposition itself following a period of lackluster sales and a falling share price. New group CEO Dave Lewis, who took the reins at the beginning of the year, told investors in February that the North America market is challenged.

"Our portfolio needs some time and investment to make it more competitive," Lewis said. The company said it was slashing its shareholder dividend as it looks to plough funds into a turnaround plan aimed at arresting a slide in sales volumes, a trend that has also weighed on Diageo's peers in the booze industry.

The company has in recent months inked deals to offload some assets, including its Kenyan business and a cricket team in India. But it could also broaden some parts of its portfolio, Lewis said. Analysts have suggested some U.S. mid-market brands might suit the group's shift in strategy.

The company is also hoping for a boost to North America sales this year from the soccer world championship due to be played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico over the summer.


Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

04-01-26 0826ET

US private payrolls increase steadily in March, ADP says
data.description:
WASHINGTON, April 1 (Reuters) - U.S. private payrolls increased steadily in March, the ADP's national employment report showed on Wednesday.

Private employment rose by 62,000 jobs last month after an upwardly revised 66,000 gain in February. Economists polled by Reuters had forecast private employment rising by 40,000 jobs after a previously reported 63,000 increase in February. 

The ADP report is jointly developed with the Stanford Digital Economy Lab, and was published ahead of the Bureau of Labor Statistics' more comprehensive and closely watched employment report for March on Friday. ADP has been a poor predictor of the BLS' private payrolls estimate.

Nonfarm payrolls likely rebounded by 60,000 after decreasing 92,000 in February, a Reuters survey of economists predicted, as a strike by healthcare workers ended and temperatures warmed up.

Private payrolls are forecast to have increased by 70,000 after slumping 86,000 in the prior month. The unemployment rate is expected to have held steady at 4.4%.

(Reporting by Lucia Mutikani; Editing by Chizu Nomiyama)

data.title: Legrand SA (ENXTPA:LR) agreed to acquire Guangzhou Nandun Communication Equipment Co., Ltd.
data.description:
Legrand SA (ENXTPA:LR) agreed to acquire Guangzhou Nandun Communication Equipment Co., Ltd. on April 1, 2026. In related separate transaction, Legrand also acquires TES in United Kingdom.

For the period ending December 31, 2025, Guangzhou Nandun Communication Equipment Co., Ltd. reported total revenue of ?60 million. Keydak employs more than 330 people.

Transaction is subject to customary closing conditions.

data.title: Chery and Jaguar Land Rover Launches Freelander Premium EV Brand Through Strategic Collaboration
data.description:
Chery Group and Jaguar Land Rover jointly developed the new-era FREELANDER as part of their successful 12-year joint venture. It represents a new model of forward-looking strategic collaboration, combining Chery?s advanced Chinese technology with Jaguar Land Rover's expertise in world class design and the spirit and DNA of the original 1997 FREELANDER. FREELANDER has evolved into an independent premium EV technology brand, ushering in a new era of global premium redefinition.

FREELANDER features its brand new architecture and collaborations with top-tier partners including Huawei, CATL, and Qualcomm. The brand plans to launch a family of new models within five years, in key global markets. This year, it aims to establish a high-quality service network in China with 100 stores across 60 cities.

FREELANDER has established a fully integrated operations system including: Global Headquarters in Shanghai, Shanghai FREELANDER Design Hub, Suzhou R&D Institute and Changshu CJLR intelligent Manufacturing Facility, with nearly 5,000 global experts. This layout allows the brand shape the global premium EV market from China to the world. FREELANDER?s global brand strategy comprises a new approach: New Positioning: Into a fully independent brand with standalone operations.

New Energy: premium SUV EVs, all models electrified and intelligently equipped. New Business Model: The new model of collaboration leverages both parties' complementary strengths.

Boeing, Defense Dept Agree to 7-Year Framework to Triple PAC-3 Seeker Production
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall


Boeing and the Defense Department agreed to a seven-year framework to triple production of the Patriot Advanced Capability-3 missile seekers.

The aerospace-and-defense company said it will immediately begin ramping up production with the Defense Department and Lockheed Martin, the prime contractor for PAC-3 missile segment enhancement. The parties will negotiate toward a multiyear contract award later in the year, they said.

Boeing said it had already invested more than $200 million since 2024 in the Huntsville, Ala., facility that makes PAC-3 seekers. "The framework paves the way for additional, cash-neutral Boeing investments throughout the production value stream, providing certainty to meet growing demand," the company said.

The push to increase production is part of the Pentagon's "Arsenal of Freedom" initiative to strengthen the U.S. defense industrial base, Boeing said.

"The administration's acquisition transformation strategy empowers industry to invest and accelerate the delivery of complex systems at scale and strengthen the department's arsenal of freedom," said Steve Parker, Boeing's chief executive of defense, space and security, referring to an initiative to strengthen the U.S. defense industrial base. "With this framework, we'll be able to produce and deliver more advanced seekers and enhance our military's advantage."

Boeing shares were up 2% to $202.94 in premarket trading.


Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

04-01-26 0814ET
